generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components //
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary actuator converting electrical energy (current I_M) into mechanical torque (τ_M) to rotate the gear system of the MABI MAX 100 robot's first axis. Driven by control signals from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gear component transmitting rotational energy between the permanent magnet synchronous motor and cycloidal gearbox in the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision gearbox providing speed reduction and torque multiplication for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot, handling working range [-90°, 90°] and max angular velocity 75°/s."
]

instance Sinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller processing position, speed, and acceleration commands to generate motor control signals (I_M) for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor providing real-time angular displacement (r_M) and velocity measurements for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind the gearbox providing angular displacement (r_G) and velocity measurements for backlash and positional uncertainty analysis of the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderData
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor current sensor measuring electrical input current (I_M) for torque estimation and friction analysis in the first axis system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentSensorData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance CurrentSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

instance MqttStreamedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoderData, SecondaryEncoderData, CurrentSensorData
    base:desc "MQTT-based real-time streaming of time-series data (I_M, encoder readings) from physical twin to digital twin for friction analysis and control optimization."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) defining structural composition of first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) in the MABI MAX 100 robot."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) describing flow definitions (rotational energy, electrical current) between components of the first axis system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance MotorBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating motor behavior converting current (I_M) to mechanical torque (τ_M) for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearboxBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating gearbox behavior calculating output torque (τ_G) and angular velocity (ω) for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous characteristics to compute friction losses in the first axis gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven friction model implementing Stribeck effect for dynamic friction analysis in the first axis system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Nonlinear friction model implementing Dahl model for stick-slip behavior analysis in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Advanced LuGre friction model capturing detailed dynamic friction characteristics for predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw motor current measurements (I_M) transmitted via MQTT for torque estimation and friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModelingService
]

instance MeasuredMotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Angular position measurements (r_M) from primary encoder for real-time motion tracking and deviation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotionAccuracyService
]

instance MeasuredGearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Angular position measurements (r_G) from secondary encoder enabling backlash and positional uncertainty analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotionAccuracyService
]

instance SimulatedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated motor torque (τ̂_M) generated by friction models for comparison with measured torque outputs."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MqttStreamedData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModelingService, DataVisualizationService
]

instance SimulatedGearboxTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated gearbox output torque (τ̂_G) calculated by friction models for motion accuracy assessment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MqttStreamedData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModelingService, DataVisualizationService
]

instance SimulatedOutputTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated output torque (τ̂_out) representing ideal behavior without friction for deviation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MqttStreamedData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RootCauseAnalysisService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) tool using SysML for structural (BDD) and behavioral modeling of the first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModelingService, BehavioralModelingService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink environment executing friction and dynamics models for real-time comparison with measured data."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, MotionAccuracyService
]

instance MqttDataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time streaming of sensor data (I_M, encoder readings) from physical twin to digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShellEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Potential standardized data exchange framework (AAS) for future implementation of standardized digital twin interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:enables StandardizedDataExchangeService
]

instance CustomVisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom dashboard providing unified display of simulated vs. measured data traces with semantic linking capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService, RootCauseAnalysisService
]

// C6: Services
instance StructuralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML Block Definition Diagrams for structural representation of first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:provides AxisComponentStructureInsight
]

instance BehavioralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing SysML Internal Block Diagrams for behavioral flow definitions between axis components."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentInteractionInsight
]

instance FrictionModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously identifying static, kinetic, and viscous friction characteristics using white-box and black-box models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionParameterUpdateAction, WearPatternDetectionInsight
]

instance MotionAccuracyService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service adjusting control algorithms in real-time to compensate for friction-induced positional errors (up to 30%)."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAlgorithmOptimizationAction, PositionalAccuracyImprovementInsight
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing real-time MQTT streaming of time-series data from physical twin to digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing unified dashboard display of simulated vs. measured data traces for multi-physics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualDataComparisonInsight
]

instance RootCauseAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service comparing simulated (τ̂_out) and measured (τ_out) torque outputs to identify positional uncertainties."
    DTDFVocab:provides RootCauseDiagnosisInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing friction model degradation to identify wear patterns in gearboxes."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance StandardizedDataExchangeService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Potential future service implementing standardized data exchange protocols via Asset Administration Shells."
    DTDFVocab:provides StandardizedInterfaceInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time automatic adjustment of friction model parameters (e.g., Coulomb coefficients) based on operational data deviations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlAlgorithmOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic refinement of robot motion commands to compensate for friction-induced inaccuracies in the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic generation of maintenance recommendations based on detected wear patterns in gearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AxisComponentStructureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural representation of first axis components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) as defined in SysML BDD."
]

instance ComponentInteractionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Behavioral flow definitions between components showing rotational energy and electrical current interactions."
]

instance FrictionParameterUpdateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current values of friction model parameters (Coulomb, viscous coefficients) derived from operational data."
]

instance WearPatternDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified wear patterns in gearbox components through friction model degradation analysis."
]

instance PositionalAccuracyImprovementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantified improvement in positional accuracy achieved through friction-compensated control algorithms."
]

instance VisualDataComparisonInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Unified visualization of simulated vs. measured data traces enabling multi-physics-aware analysis."
]

instance RootCauseDiagnosisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Root cause analysis results comparing simulated (τ̂_out) and measured (τ_out) torque outputs for positional uncertainties."
]

instance DataTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Complete traceability of data traces linking physical measurements to their digital twin model representations."
]

instance StandardizedInterfaceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Potential standardized data exchange interfaces enabled by future Asset Administration Shell implementation."
]

// C18: Environment
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains Sinumerik840DslController,
                PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor,
                GearWheel,
                CycloidalGearbox,
                MotorEncoder,
                SecondaryEncoder,
                CurrentSensor,
                SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram,
                SysMLInternalBlockDiagram,
                MotorBehaviorModel,
                GearboxBehaviorModel,
                CoulombViscousFrictionModel,
                StribeckFrictionModel,
                DahlFrictionModel,
                LuGreFrictionModel,
                SysMLModelingEnabler,
                SimulinkSimulationEnabler,
                MqttDataIntegrationEnabler,
                AssetAdministrationShellEnabler,
                CustomVisualizationDashboard,
                StructuralModelingService,
                BehavioralModelingService,
                FrictionModelingService,
                MotionAccuracyService,
                DataStreamingService,
                DataVisualizationService,
                RootCauseAnalysisService,
                PredictiveMaintenanceService,
                StandardizedDataExchangeService,
                MeasuredCurrentData,
                MeasuredMotorPositionData,
                MeasuredGearboxPositionData,
                SimulatedMotorTorqueData,
                SimulatedGearboxTorqueData,
                SimulatedOutputTorqueData
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates at **real-time or near-real-time scales**, with data synchronization occurring at the **frequency of sensor measurements** (not explicitly stated but inferred from MQTT streaming). The friction models are dynamically updated during operation to account for **wear, temperature, and load variations**, ensuring continuous adaptation. The DT-to-PT synchronization is **event-driven** (e.g., during motion commands) and **time-aligned** (via timestamped data points)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is **purpose-driven**, centered on **friction analysis** for the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot. It integrates:
   - **Physical Layer**: Motor, gearboxes, encoders, and Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller.
   - **Virtual Layer**: Digital shadow with **engineering, simulation, and data calculation models** linked via UML class diagrams.
   - **Processing Layer**: Simulation components (e.g., *Simulation_M*, *Simulation_GW*, *Simulation_GB*) that compute friction-adjusted outputs (*τ̂_M*, *τ̂_G*, *τ̂_out*) from input data traces.
   The constellation follows a **one-way data flow** (PT → DT) with **no closed-loop control** (DT does not directly command the PT)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT covers the **operational phase** of the robot’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** (real-world) behavior rather than design or manufacturing stages. It integrates **engineering models** (structural and behavioral) with **live operational data** to analyze friction-induced inaccuracies. The DT is not used for **as-designed** (ideal) or **as-manufactured** validation but rather for **runtime optimization** during machining tasks."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
   - **Model Verification**: Simulink models are validated against **manufacturer-provided dynamics parameters** and **real-world sensor data**.
   - **Uncertainty Handling**: Friction models account for **parameter variability** (e.g., load, temperature) via **data-driven (black-box) and analytical (white-box) approaches**.
   - **Error Mitigation**: Deviations between simulated (*τ̂_M*, *τ̂_G*) and measured (*τ_M*, *τ_G*) data are analyzed to **identify friction losses** and adjust control algorithms.
   - **Traceability**: Each data point is linked to its **source (sensor/model)**, **processing component**, and **computational models** (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous friction) for auditable validation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT connects to the PT via:
   - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for **real-time data streaming** of sensor inputs (*I_M*, encoder readings) at unspecified but high frequency (assumed to match control loop timing).
   - **Data Format**: **Floating-point arrays** for torque (*τ*) and angular velocity (*ω*) with **timestamped metadata**.
   - **Architecture**: **Client-server model** where the DT (server) receives data from the PT (client) via the Siemens controller. No bidirectional protocols (e.g., OPC UA) are mentioned."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted on a **local or cloud-based infrastructure** (not specified) but is designed for **real-time execution** using **MathWorks MATLAB Simulink** for model computation. The dashboard for visualization is likely deployed as a **web-based application** (technology unspecified) integrated with the MQTT broker. No details on **scalability, redundancy, or deployment topology** (e.g., edge vs. cloud) are provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to:
   - **SysML** (OMG standard) for **engineering models** (BDDs, IBDs).
   - **MQTT** (IETF standard) for **real-time data exchange**.
   - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** is referenced as a **potential future enabler** for standardized data modeling (ISO/IEC 42040).
   No explicit compliance with **OPC UA, Industry 4.0, or IIoT standards** is described."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety considerations include:
   - **Data Integrity**: MQTT ensures **encrypted data transmission** (assumed, though not explicitly stated) to prevent tampering with sensor inputs (*I_M*, encoder data).
   - **Operational Safety**: The DT is **read-only** (no closed-loop control), reducing risks of **unintended PT actuation**.
   - **Access Control**: No details on **authentication/authorization** for DT access or data ownership are provided.
   - **Safety Cell Integration**: The robot’s **safety cell** limits the working range to [-90°, 90°], mitigating risks of collisions or misalignment during friction analysis."
]
